exemplar.title: M25 #6: Does acquiring knowledge destroy our sense of wonder? Discuss with reference to two areas of knowledge.

detailedExemplarData.title: M25 #6: Does acquiring knowledge destroy our sense of wonder? Discuss with reference to two areas of knowledge.

strengths: Sustained, focused engagement with the prescribed title and clear links to the Natural Sciences and History AOKs.
Arguments are coherent and well supported by specific, relevant examples (e.g. bioluminescence, aurora borealis, discovery of DNA structure, James Cookâs voyages, ANZAC Day).
Key terms (âacquiring knowledge,â âsense of wonderâ) are explicitly defined, providing a solid conceptual framework.
Effective use of quotations and analogies (Feynman, Wheeler, magicâtrick analogy) adds authority and clarity to the analysis.
Awareness and evaluation of multiple perspectives (scientific vs emotional, romantic vs revisionist) demonstrates balanced, pluralistic reasoning.
weaknesses: Depth of evaluation is limited; counterclaims and implications are not explored as rigorously as required for the top band.
Occasional descriptive passages (e.g. overview of the scientific method, broad history introduction) lack explicit linkage back to the sense of wonder.
Structural issues â a lengthy definitional introduction delays the thesis and there is no clear roadmap early in the essay.
Inconsistent academic tone and citation (use of firstâperson, reliance on a blog citation) undermines formality and rigour.
The conclusion summarises effectively but does not extend to wider implications or a followâon knowledge question.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay clearly meets the 7â8 band descriptors: it maintains sustained focus on the title, links arguments effectively to two AOKs, and supports claims with apt, concrete examples. The student shows genuine awareness of different points of view and uses a strong conceptual framework. These strengths justify a high mark within the band. However, the analysis is not yet at the sustained sophistication and evaluative depth required for a 9â10, and minor structural and stylistic issues (lengthy introduction, descriptive tangents, citation inconsistencies) prevent it from reaching the very top. Given the official examinerâs total of 8 and the essayâs performance across the strands, an 8 is an appropriate reflection of its overall quality.

annotations.comment: This long definitional paragraph delays the presentation of an explicit thesis. Consider condensing definitions and introducing your central claim earlier to maintain reader engagement.
A clear topic sentence introduces the Natural Sciences section, effectively signalling the transition into the first area of knowledge discussion.
This overview of the scientific method lacks an explicit link to the sense of wonder; strengthen the analysis by directly tying each methodological step back to its impact on wonder.
The examples of bioluminescence and the aurora borealis ground the argument in concrete phenomena, enhancing reader understanding of how scientific explanation can affect wonder.
The student restates the prescribed title verbatim, demonstrating direct engagement with the knowledge question and ensuring the essay remains focused on the core TOK prompt.
This paragraph would benefit from incorporating a counterclaimâperhaps a scientistâs reflection on emotional engagementâto deepen the evaluative dimension of the argument.
The incorporation of Richard Feynmanâs quote powerfully illustrates how scientific insight can enrich wonder, lending authoritative weight to the analysis.
Explicitly identifying the two areas of knowledge (Natural Sciences and History) aligns the response clearly with the title and provides a focused framework for the subsequent discussion.
The student offers clear operational definitions of âacquiring knowledgeâ and âsense of wonder,â establishing a solid conceptual foundation for the essayâs arguments.
The discussion remains descriptive here without clear signposting of the essayâs structure or how each AOK will be addressed; a brief roadmap would improve coherence.
This concise closing sentence neatly wraps up the Natural Sciences section and signals an effective transition to the History discussion.
The magicâtrick analogy effectively illustrates how demystification can transform emotional responses, adding clarity and engaging the reader with a relatable comparison.
This transitional sentence cleanly shifts the discussion to the alternative perspective, maintaining logical flow between contrasting lines of argument.
The DNA doubleâhelix example compellingly shows that scientific discovery often uncovers new questions, reinforcing the cyclical nature of wonder and inquiry.
To deepen evaluation, consider addressing ethical or societal implications of genetic knowledge, which would add nuance to the discussion of wonder.
Reliance on a blog citation for Feynmanâs perspective undermines academic rigor; seek a primary source or peerâreviewed account to strengthen credibility.
The History introduction is broad and descriptive; consider posing a specific historical knowledge question to sharpen focus and guide readers.
The James Cook example effectively situates the discussion in a concrete historical context, illustrating how deeper knowledge can alter collective perceptions.
Use of firstâperson plural (âwe,â âmy classâ) introduces informality; maintain academic objectivity by framing observations impersonally.
John Archibald Wheelerâs quote introduces a valuable epistemological insight on ignorance, enriching the theoretical depth of the History section.
The conclusion effectively summarises both AOKs but stops short of exploring wider implications or proposing a followâon knowledge question; consider extending this evaluative dimension.
The phrasing âstripping away the mysteryâ repeats previous terminology; varying language and sentence structure would enhance stylistic clarity.
The ANZAC Day example broadens the discussion to a collective historical wonder, demonstrating how community narratives can sustain and deepen shared curiosity.
This anecdotal account would benefit from integration of secondary sources or historiographical commentary to substantiate claims about communal wonder.
Acknowledging the individual nature of wonder reflects strong TOK awareness of the knowerâs perspective, reinforcing the essayâs balanced, pluralistic stance.
Bibliography entries show varied formats and incomplete details; adopt a consistent citation style (e.g. MLA or APA) and include full publication information.

The discussion is weakly connected to the title. While there may be links to the areas of knowledge, any relevant points are descriptive or consist only of unsupported assertions.

The discussion is connected to the title and makes superficial or limited links to areas of knowledge. The discussion is largely descriptive. Limited arguments are offered but they are unclear and are not supported by effective examples.

of view.

The discussion is focused on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and supported by examples. There is awareness and some evaluation of different points of view.

The discussion has a sustained focus on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and effectively supported by specific examples. The implications of arguments are considered. There is clear awareness and evaluation of different points of view.
